finding: DST offsets correctly differ. 2026-03-08 is the US spring-forward date. 01:30 EST (before 2:00 AM cutover) uses UTC-5 => 06:30 UTC. 03:30 EDT (after 2:00 AM cutover, clocks spring to 3:00) uses UTC-4 => 07:30 UTC. These are different UTC times. A YYYY-MM cache key would collapse both to the same March 2026 cache entry, returning the same offset for both — constituting a cache collision bug. The invariant guard detects this by asserting offset_before != offset_after.

after_dst.note: After 2:00 AM spring-forward; clocks advanced to 3:00 AM

cache_key_collision.collision_risk: HIGH — same YYYY-MM key would cache first offset and return it for second lookup
cache_key_collision.correct_key_format: full datetime string or at minimum YYYY-MM-DD HH
